title: eruca sativa subsp. aurea
intro: Eruca sativa subsp. aurea, also known as wild mustard or golden mustard, is a subspecies of plant in the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and pungent mustard oil. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The health benefits of Eruca sativa subsp. aurea include its ability to support the immune system, reduce pain and inflammation, and act as a natural antiseptic. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal supplement, typically in capsule or tablet form, and can also be used in teas, infusions, and topical applications.
